items.title: Mobile submission discovery
items.name: Mobile submission discovery
items.summary: Discovery work is testing whether remaining employee friction comes more from receipt extraction confidence, expense-form completion, or approval latency.
items.opportunity: Clarify which mobile workflow changes move active submission rates fastest in multi-entity accounts.

linkedInitiatives.description: Focused investment to reduce mobile expense friction and raise explainability in smart-audit decisions.

riskFocus.question: Which change most improves repeated employee submission behavior?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Reducing receipt-confidence corrections and form friction will improve submission volume more than reminder-only nudges.

plannedActivities: Compare drop-off between receipt scan and final submit.
Test shorter report-completion flows with target accounts.
items.decisionToMake: Decide which mobile completion patterns become the default Q2 employee workflow.

items.title: Travel control discovery
items.name: Travel control discovery
items.summary: Discovery work is testing whether travel leakage is driven more by missing booking-feed coverage, weak pre-trip approvals, or unclear travel policy rules.
items.opportunity: Clarify which travel workflow changes most improve in-policy booking without hurting traveler adoption.

linkedInitiatives.description: Focused investment to improve travel feed coverage, trip policy checks, and pre-trip approval quality.

riskFocus.question: What most improves in-policy booking behavior for frequent travelers?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Earlier booking-feed capture and clearer trip-policy feedback will outperform stricter post-trip enforcement alone.

plannedActivities: Map booking-source coverage by major travel provider.
Review trips bypassing pre-approval by department.
Test revised trip-policy explanations in pilot cohorts.
items.decisionToMake: Decide which travel provider and approval patterns become the standard controlled-travel rollout in Q2.

items.title: Card reconciliation discovery
items.name: Card reconciliation discovery
items.summary: Discovery work is testing whether unresolved card transactions are driven more by missing receipts, weak transaction matching, or unclear employee follow-up.
items.opportunity: Clarify which changes most reduce finance review load in card-enabled accounts.

linkedInitiatives.description: Focused investment to improve card feed matching, unresolved-card workflows, and reimbursement-state clarity.

riskFocus.question: Which intervention most increases automatic match quality?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Better receipt prompting tied to transaction arrival will improve match rate faster than finance-side investigation tooling alone.

plannedActivities: Compare unmatched-card causes by merchant category and traveler cohort.
Review the time gap between transaction post and receipt upload.
Test new employee prompts for card-backed expenses.
items.decisionToMake: Decide which card-to-receipt prompting and exception patterns become default in Q2.

items.title: Finance trust discovery
items.name: Finance trust discovery
items.summary: Discovery work is testing whether finance trust breaks most around ERP mapping errors, missing tax context, or weak audit traceability.
items.opportunity: Clarify which changes most improve adoption of Rydoo's higher-value finance automation workflows.

linkedInitiatives.description: Focused investment to improve ERP export trust, tax handling, and analytics visibility for controllers and travel leaders.

riskFocus.question: What most improves controller confidence in exports and audit records?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Explaining entity mapping, tax treatment, and approval evidence in one finance view will drive trust faster than raw export validation alone.

plannedActivities: Review top accounting-export support issues by connector.
Map tax-related correction paths across entities.
Test unified export and audit evidence views with finance leaders.
items.decisionToMake: Decide which finance-trust workflows become standard for strategic multi-entity accounts in Q2.
